title: ” she said.
content:
” she said.
“She doesn’t
come for herself—she has a little grandson. She makes these trips just as regular as clockwork”
(Welty, 1941, p. 4)【20】. This passage confirms that Phoenix’s journey is not a one-time struggle
but a continuous act of devotion. Her old age and failing memory add to the gravity of her
sacrifice, making her perseverance even more profound.
Conclusion
Through the symbolic use of Phoenix’s name and the worn path, Welty effectively conveys
themes of perseverance and sacrifice. Phoenix Jackson embodies the selflessness of
unconditional love, as she repeatedly overcomes obstacles for the well-being of her grandson.
Despite facing both physical and societal hardships, she remains undeterred, much like the
mythical phoenix rising again and again. Welty’s use of symbolism transforms A Worn Path into
a powerful meditation on endurance and love.
References
Welty, E. (1941). A Worn Path.
